title: MavenGate Attack Hijacks Java and Android Dependencies via Expired Domain Purchase Affecting 18% of Maven Central
description: The MavenGate supply chain attack allowed attackers to hijack Java and Android library dependencies by purchasing expired domains corresponding to abandoned Maven groupIds, enabling injection of malicious code into applications built by Google, Facebook, Signal, and Amazon with 6,170 of 33,938 analyzed domains found vulnerable.
label: AV-509

title: MavenGate Enables Malicious Plugin Injection into Java Build Process via Abandoned GroupId Takeover
description: The MavenGate attack demonstrated that beyond dependency hijacking, attackers who gain control of an abandoned Maven groupId can inject malicious Gradle and Maven build plugins that execute arbitrary code during the build process of all applications using the compromised groupId.
label: AV-509

title: Java-Class-Hijack Attack Injects Malicious Class via Maven Dependency Packaging Order to Control Database Logic
description: The Java-Class-Hijack supply chain attack exploits Maven's depth-first dependency packaging order and JVM classpath resolution to inject a malicious class with the same fully qualified name as a legitimate one, demonstrated by compromising the Corona-Warn-App's database connection logic.
label: AV-509

title: Maven-Hijack Exploits Dependency Resolution Order to Override Core Application Behavior Without Modifying Source
description: Maven-Hijack attacks exploit Maven's depth-first search dependency packaging and Java's classpath resolution mechanism to silently override core application behavior by injecting malicious classes into transitive dependencies without modifying main codebase or library names.
label: AV-509

title: Artifactory Maven Proxy Artifact Poisoning via CVE-2024-6915 Allows Serving Malicious spring-boot-starter-web
description: CVE-2024-6915 in JFrog Artifactory allowed attackers to poison Maven proxy repositories by saving any HTTP response from a remote endpoint as an arbitrary artifact, enabling malicious versions of commonly used libraries like spring-boot-starter-web to be served to downstream builds.
label: AV-509

title: GitHub Supply Chain Attack Uses Stolen Browser Cookies to Take Over Developer Accounts and Commit Malicious Code
description: A GitHub supply chain attack leveraged stolen browser cookies to hijack developer accounts, using compromised accounts to contribute malicious code with verified commits and publish malicious packages to PyPI linked to popular GitHub projects via a fake pypihosted.org mirror.
label: AV-200

title: GitHub Supply Chain Attack Installs Keylogger Capturing Passwords Personal Messages and Financial Details
description: A complex GitHub supply chain attack installed keylogger malware on developer systems that logged all keystrokes including passwords, personal messages, and financial details, while also stealing browser cookies, autofill data, credit cards, and login credentials from Chrome, Edge, Opera, and Yandex.
label: AV-400

title: Malicious PyPI Package Decrypts and Steals Discord Tokens to Hijack Developer Discord Accounts
description: A malicious PyPI package in the GitHub supply chain attack campaign accessed Discord application data directories, decrypted locally stored Discord authentication tokens, and exfiltrated them to attacker infrastructure enabling complete takeover of victim Discord accounts.
label: AV-400

title: Supply Chain Malware Steals Cryptocurrency Wallets and Telegram Data from Compromised Developer Systems
description: A malicious package distributed through a compromised GitHub supply chain attack stole cryptocurrency wallet files and Telegram session data from infected developer machines, combining financial theft with communication account compromise.
label: AV-400

title: Malicious Supply Chain Package Steals Instagram Session Tokens from Compromised Developer Machines
description: A malicious package distributed through a typosquatted Python mirror registered as files.pypihosted.org stole Instagram session tokens from developer machines, targeting social media authentication credentials alongside financial and communication data.
label: AV-400

title: Malicious Rust Crate rustdecimal Typosquats rust_decimal Financial Library on Crates.io
description: The malicious Rust crate rustdecimal typosquatted the popular rust_decimal financial calculation library on crates.io, targeting Rust developers working on financial applications and attempting to exfiltrate sensitive configuration data from systems where it was installed.
label: AV-200

title: Malicious Rust Crate ferris-says-rs Typosquats ferris-says on Crates.io Targeting Beginners
description: A malicious Rust crate named ferris-says-rs typosquatted the popular ferris-says learning tool on crates.io, specifically targeting Rust beginners who are likely to install tutorial packages without careful verification of package names.
label: AV-200

title: Rust Cargo Dependency Confusion Attack via Crates.io and Private Registry Name Collision
description: Dependency confusion attacks targeting the Rust Cargo ecosystem exploit name collisions between private internal crates and public crates.io packages, causing Cargo's dependency resolution to automatically prefer public malicious crates over private internal ones when version numbers are higher.
label: AV-509

title: event-stream npm Package Compromise Targets Copay Bitcoin Wallet to Steal Cryptocurrency Funds
description: The event-stream npm package was compromised by a new maintainer who added the flatmap-stream dependency containing obfuscated code that specifically targeted the Copay Bitcoin wallet application to steal funds from wallets with balances exceeding 100 Bitcoin.
label: AV-400

title: Malicious npm Package Installs Hidden Cryptominer Using Victim CPU Resources for Monero Mining
description: Multiple malicious npm packages documented in security research installed hidden cryptocurrency mining software that silently consumed victim CPU and GPU resources to mine Monero cryptocurrency for attackers while running in the background of developer systems.
label: AV-400

title: Malicious npm Package Uses DNS Queries to Exfiltrate Stolen Credentials Bypassing HTTP Monitoring
description: A malicious npm package documented in supply chain security research used DNS query encoding to exfiltrate stolen credentials and environment variables, bypassing HTTP and HTTPS network monitoring tools by encoding sensitive data as subdomain labels of attacker-controlled DNS queries.
label: AV-400

title: Malicious PyPI Package Encodes AWS Credentials into DNS TXT Queries to Evade Data Loss Prevention
description: A malicious PyPI package used DNS TXT record queries to exfiltrate AWS access keys and secret tokens by encoding them as base64 strings in DNS query hostnames, evading enterprise Data Loss Prevention systems that monitor HTTP traffic but not DNS queries.
label: AV-400

title: Malicious npm Package Acts as Ransomware Dropper Encrypting Developer Project Files After Installation
description: Multiple malicious npm packages documented in security research acted as ransomware droppers that encrypted developer project files and local code repositories after installation, demanding cryptocurrency ransom payments and causing significant development disruption.
label: AV-400

title: Malicious npm Package Erases Entire Codebase After Installation Causing Catastrophic Data Loss
description: Several malicious npm packages documented by Checkmarx, ReversingLabs, Safety, and Socket erased entire developer codebases after installation, performing recursive deletion of project directories and causing catastrophic and irreversible data loss to affected developers.
label: AV-400

title: Malicious RubyGems and npm Packages Steal Telegram API Tokens from Developer Environments
description: Multiple malicious RubyGems and npm packages discovered by Checkmarx and Socket drained and exfiltrated Telegram API tokens from developer environments, enabling attackers to access Telegram Bot accounts and message histories of affected developers.
label: AV-400

title: Sonatype Identifies Combosquatting npm Packages Combining Two Legitimate Library Names to Deceive Developers
description: Sonatype researchers identified combosquatting npm packages that combined the names of two popular legitimate libraries such as react-dom-router or webpack-babel-loader, exploiting developer familiarity with both component names to increase installation credibility and bypass name-based detection.
label: AV-200

title: Malicious PyPI Package Uses Soundsquatting to Target Developers Installing Packages by Voice Search or Autocomplete
description: Security researchers documented soundsquatting attacks on PyPI where malicious packages used phonetically similar names to legitimate libraries, specifically targeting developers using voice search or IDE autocomplete features that suggest packages based on pronunciation similarity.
label: AV-200

title: Malicious npm Package Uses Internationalized Domain Name Homograph Attack to Impersonate Trusted Registry
description: A malicious npm package combined IDN homograph attacks with typosquatting by creating package names using Unicode characters that render identically to ASCII in some display contexts, impersonating trusted packages in ways that bypassed text-based security scanning tools.
label: AV-200

title: Malicious PyPI Package Impersonates Popular Security Tool bandit to Target Security-Conscious Developers
description: A malicious PyPI package impersonated the popular bandit Python security linting tool, specifically targeting security-conscious developers who actively install security tools, exploiting the irony that developers installing security tools are particularly attractive targets for credential theft.
label: AV-200

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters Affect Kotlin Android Applications Enabling Hidden Backdoors in Mobile Apps
description: Security researchers confirmed that the Trojan Source vulnerability affects Kotlin source code used for Android development, where hidden Unicode bidirectional characters can alter conditional logic and string handling in mobile applications in ways invisible to code reviewers using standard Android Studio.
label: AV-300

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in PHP Applications Enable Hidden SQL Injection and Authentication Bypass
description: The PHPCSStandards project documented Trojan Source attacks in PHP where hidden Unicode bidirectional characters enable invisible SQL injection payloads and authentication bypass logic embedded in PHP web application code that appears completely safe during manual and automated code review.
label: AV-300

title: Trojan Source Vulnerability in TypeScript Compiler Enables Hidden Logic in Typed Code with Full Type Safety Appearance
description: The Trojan Source research confirmed TypeScript is vulnerable to Unicode bidirectional character attacks where hidden characters can alter the behavior of TypeScript code while maintaining complete type safety appearance, causing TypeScript's static type checker to pass malicious code as safe.
label: AV-300

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters Embedded in GitHub README and Documentation Files Deliver Malicious Instructions
description: Security researchers documented Trojan Source attacks using Unicode characters embedded in GitHub README markdown files and documentation, causing rendered documentation to display different installation or configuration instructions from the raw markdown source visible to repository maintainers.
label: AV-300

title: Trojan Source Affects C# .NET Applications Enabling Hidden Logic in Enterprise Software Development
description: The original Trojan Source research confirmed C# and the .NET ecosystem are vulnerable to Unicode bidirectional character attacks, with particular concern for enterprise software development where C# applications often handle sensitive financial and healthcare data.
label: AV-300

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in Jupyter Notebook Cells Enable Hidden Data Exfiltration in Data Science Workflows
description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks embedded in Jupyter notebook code cells where hidden Unicode characters alter data processing logic, enabling silent exfiltration of sensitive training datasets and model outputs in data science and machine learning workflows.
label: AV-300

title: Jenkins Groovy Pipeline Script Injection via Unsanitized SCM Checkout Enables Arbitrary Command Execution
description: Jenkins pipeline poisoning attacks exploit unsanitized SCM checkout operations where Jenkinsfile changes from untrusted branches are executed with full pipeline permissions, allowing attackers to inject arbitrary Groovy code that executes with Jenkins agent credentials and workspace access.
label: AV-410

title: GitLab CI Pipeline Poisoning via Include Directive Loading Malicious External Configuration
description: GitLab CI pipeline poisoning attacks exploit the include directive that loads external YAML configuration files, where attackers modify included configuration sources to inject malicious pipeline stages that execute with the target repository's CI/CD tokens and deployment credentials.
label: AV-410

title: Compromised Python pytest Plugin Injects Malicious Test Fixtures Executing During CI Test Runs
description: A compromised Python pytest plugin injected malicious test fixtures that executed credential-stealing code during CI test runs, exploiting the elevated trust that test frameworks receive during build processes to exfiltrate secrets from CI environments.
label: AV-410

title: Malicious VS Code Extension Harvests Authentication Tokens from Developer Workstations via IDE Plugin Supply Chain
description: A malicious VS Code extension distributed through the Visual Studio Code marketplace harvested GitHub authentication tokens, SSH keys, and cloud credentials from developer workstations, demonstrating IDE plugin supply chains as an undermonitored vector for developer credential theft.
label: AV-410

title: Compromised ESLint npm Package Transforms Linting Tool into Credential Theft Vector Across Development Pipelines
description: The compromised eslint-scope npm package demonstrated how widely-used linting and static analysis tools can be transformed into credential theft vectors, affecting all CI pipelines that run linting as part of their build process and gaining access to npm authentication tokens.
label: AV-410

title: Rust Cargo Workspace Dependency Confusion via Local Path Override and Registry Fallback Exploitation
description: Dependency confusion attacks targeting Rust Cargo workspaces exploit the interaction between local path dependencies and registry fallback resolution, where incorrectly configured Cargo.toml workspace members can resolve dependencies from public crates.io instead of intended local paths.
label: AV-509

title: CVE-2024-5083 in Sonatype Nexus 2 Enables Stored XSS Leading to Maven Artifact Poisoning
description: CVE-2024-5083 in Sonatype Nexus 2 documented a stored XSS vulnerability in Maven proxy repository management that could be exploited to poison artifact caching, causing malicious artifacts to be served to downstream Java applications fetching dependencies through the compromised Nexus instance.
label: AV-509

title: CVE-2024-36115 in Reposilite Maven Repository Enables Path Traversal for Artifact Poisoning
description: CVE-2024-36115 in Reposilite Maven repository documented a path traversal vulnerability that allowed attackers to write arbitrary files to the server, enabling poisoning of cached Maven artifacts served to downstream Java builds.
label: AV-509

title: Fake pypihosted.org Python Mirror Typosquats Official pythonhosted.org to Distribute Malicious Packages
description: Attackers registered the typosquatted domain files.pypihosted.org closely resembling the official files.pythonhosted.org Python package mirror, using it to distribute malicious PyPI packages linked to popular GitHub projects and targeting developers who accidentally used the fake mirror.
label: AV-200

title: Malicious npm Package reakt Typosquats react Library Targeting React Framework Developers
description: The malicious npm package reakt used a one-character transposition to typosquat the extremely popular React JavaScript library, targeting the massive React developer ecosystem and executing credential-stealing postinstall scripts on developer machines installing what they believed was the React framework.
label: AV-200

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in Kubernetes YAML Manifests Alter Pod Security Configurations
description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks embedded in Kubernetes YAML manifests where hidden Unicode characters alter pod security context configurations, causing containers to run with elevated privileges or disabled security controls invisible to infrastructure reviewers.
label: AV-300

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in GitHub Actions YAML Alter Conditional Job Execution Logic
description: Trojan Source attacks embedded in GitHub Actions workflow YAML files use hidden Unicode characters to alter conditional job execution logic, causing if conditions on security-critical workflow steps to evaluate differently from their visual representation and bypassing manual workflow review.
label: AV-300

title: Compromised prettier Code Formatter npm Package Transforms Formatting Step into Credential Exfiltration
description: A compromised version of the prettier code formatter npm package weaponized the widely-used formatting step in development workflows, executing credential-stealing code during routine code formatting operations that occur frequently in both local development and CI pipeline runs.
label: AV-410

title: CircleCI Orb Supply Chain Compromise Injects Malicious Steps into Shared Pipeline Configurations
description: A supply chain compromise targeting CircleCI orbs injected malicious pipeline steps into widely-shared reusable CircleCI configurations, affecting all pipelines that imported the compromised orb and gaining access to environment variables and deployment credentials across multiple organizations.
label: AV-410

title: MavenGate Attacker Uses GitHub Actions Token Theft to Publish Malicious Artifacts to Maven Central
description: MavenGate attackers combined GitHub Actions token theft with expired Maven groupId takeover to publish malicious artifacts directly to Maven Central, using stolen CI credentials to authenticate package publishing and bypass manual artifact review processes.
label: AV-509

title: Supply Chain Malware Extracts Browser Autofill Data Including Saved Passwords and Credit Card Numbers
description: A malicious package distributed through a compromised GitHub supply chain systematically extracted browser autofill data from Chrome, Edge, Opera, and Yandex browsers including saved passwords, credit card numbers, and form autofill information stored in browser credential stores.
label: AV-400

title: Malicious PyPI Packages Typosquat Popular AI and LLM SDK Libraries Targeting AI Developer Ecosystem
description: A growing trend of malicious PyPI packages documented by security researchers typosquat popular AI and LLM SDK libraries including OpenAI, Anthropic, and Hugging Face client packages, targeting the rapidly growing AI developer ecosystem where developers frequently install new packages.
label: AV-200

title: Malicious Rust Crate Executes Data Destruction Payload Wiping Cargo Workspace and Git Repository
description: A malicious Rust crate documented in supply chain security research executed a data destruction payload that recursively deleted the victim's Cargo workspace files and Git repository history upon compilation, causing catastrophic and irreversible loss of development work.
label: AV-400

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in Makefiles Alter Build Target Execution Order and Commands
description: Security researchers documented Trojan Source attacks in Makefile build configurations where hidden Unicode characters alter build target execution order and shell command interpretation, causing make build systems to execute different commands from what developers see when reviewing build configurations.
label: AV-300

title: Compromised Maven Build Plugin Executes Malicious Lifecycle Phase During Java Application Compilation
description: A compromised Maven build plugin injected malicious execution into the compile lifecycle phase of Java applications, stealing Maven credentials, Git configuration, and cloud provider tokens from developer workstations during routine build operations.
label: AV-410
